name: Canola Breeding Summer Associate
description: Job Description: Now hiring! Canola Breeding Summer Associate Location: Regina (Emerald Park), SK Contract Duration: 6 months (Summer 2026) Wage: $24 - $31 / Hour During peak periods in the season, overtime will be paid for evenings or weekends. We are looking for a Canola Breeding Summer Associate to join our Agricultural Solutions team in Regina, SK. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. Students will be hired and employed through a third-party contracting agency - Raise Recruiting. Opportunities are based on business needs at our sites. Candidates resumes will be presented to BASF based on requirements of the assignments being a match and fit based on experience. Applications can be considered for multiple opportunities now through March 31, 2026. If you are selected, you will be contacted about the summer employment opportunity directly. As a Canola Breeding Summer Associate, you create chemistry by... Being involved in field trials for local and global breeding programs with the goal of developing hybrids that provide the highest return to growers. Being involved with field activities relating to variety development and registration from seeding to harvest including seed set up, land preparation, seeding, pest control, pollination management, plot maintenance, parental development, harvesting and supervision of casual employees as required. Being involved in crop health diagnosis through daily monitoring of field and research plots and data collection. Working in a group setting or independently dependant on the task at hand. If you have... Breeding and Agronomic knowledge attained through pursuing your degree/diploma in Agriculture or related program/field and/or recently obtained your Degree/Diploma in Agriculture in the last 2 years. Experience in the Agriculture Industry is an asset, but not a requirement. Demonstrate problem solving skills. Ability to be flexible and modify plans as situations arise minimizing impact on results. Interest in canola development. Ability to deliver results in pursuit of both individual and team goals. Previous farm or machinery experience is an asset but not a requirement as it is a skill set that will be developed on the job. About us As the world’s leading chemical company, we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! We constantly strive to become an even better workplace. As a result of our commitment to our employees, BASF has earned various awards, including being recognized by Mediacorp in 2025 as one of Canada's Top Employers for eleventh consecutive year, one of the Greater Toronto Area's Top Employers for the twelfth year in a row, and twice being named one of Canada's Top Employers for Young People and Best Diversity Employers. We provide a fulfilling work environment with a strong emphasis on the physical and psychological safety of our employees and the communities we operate in. We are always working to form the best team with an emphasis on diversity, equity, and inclusion and through lifelong learning and development. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Job Description Footer:

name: Canola Seed Production Summer Associate
description: Job Description: Now hiring! Canola Seed Production Summer Associate Contract Duration: 4 - 6 months (Summer 2026) Location: Outlook, SK Wage: $24 - $31 / Hour During peak periods in the season, overtime will be paid for evenings or weekends. We are looking for a Canola Seed Production Summer Associate to join our Agricultural Solutions team in Outlook,SK. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. Students will be hired and employed through a third-party contracting agency - Raise Recruiting. Opportunities are based on business needs at our sites. Candidates resumes will be presented to BASF based on requirements of the assignments being a match and fit based on experience. Applications can be considered for multiple opportunities now through March 31, 2026. If you are selected, you will be contacted about the summer employment opportunity directly. As a Canola Seed Production Summer Associate, you create chemistry by... Joining our team where you will be involved in plot and field scale trials for the Certified Seed Supply team. Participating in programs evaluating innovative seed canola production methods. The main objective of the program is to improve and optimize production systems that influence seed quality and seed yield from parental lines. Being responsible for field activities related to the management and data collection from multiple sites located in Southern Alberta, in addition to the collection of data there are responsibilities that include but are not limited to the timely execution of agronomic practices such as planting, spraying, crop synchronization, mowing harvesting and sample processing. If you have... Above average communication skills and the understanding of working on team settings. Demonstrate problem solving skills/comfortable working alone from time to time. Ability to be flexible and modify plans as situations arise minimizing impact on results. Experience with farm equipment (Highly desired). Agronomic knowledge attained through pursuing your degree in Agriculture or similar program, preferably along with experience in the Agriculture or Farming Industry . About us As the world’s leading chemical company, we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! We constantly strive to become an even better workplace. As a result of our commitment to our employees, BASF has earned various awards, including being recognized by Mediacorp in 2024 as one of Canada's Top Employers for tenth consecutive year, one of the Greater Toronto Area's Top Employers for the eleventh year in a row, and twice being named one of Canada's Top Employers for Young People and Best Diversity Employers. We provide a fulfilling work environment with a strong emphasis on the physical and psychological safety of our employees and the communities we operate in. We are always working to form the best team with an emphasis on diversity, equity, and inclusion and through lifelong learning and development. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Job Description Footer:

name: Trait Development Field Summer Associate
description: Job Description: Now hiring! Trait Development Associate Contract Duration: 4 - 6 months (Summer) Location: Saskatoon, SK Wage: $24 - $31 / Hour We are looking for a Trait Development Field Associate to join our AP team in Saskatoon, SK. Come create chemistry with us! BASF’s Agricultural Solutions division connects innovation, customers, partners and agricultural experts and integrates sustainability criteria into all business decisions. We help farmers deliver the best possible outcomes, working to achieve the balance between economic, environmental and social value creation for sustainable and efficient agriculture. Students will be hired and employed through a third-party contracting agency - Raise Recruiting. Opportunities are based on business needs at our sites. Candidates resumes will be presented to BASF based on requirements of the assignments being a match and fit based on experience. Applications can be considered for multiple opportunities now through March 31, 2026. If you are selected, you will be contacted about the summer employment opportunity directly. As a Trait Development Associate , you create chemistry by being involved in ... The advancement of new traits to be developed in support of local and global programs. Field activities relating to new trait development from seeding to harvest including: Seed set up Land preparation Seeding Pest control Pollination management Plot maintenance Harvesting Supervision of casual employees (as required) Ensuring standard operating procedures Compliance monitoring and maintenance of the trial site isolation areas and post-harvest sites for the presence of prohibited species by scouting fields. Maintaining the sites for a standard expected by our organization and the Canadian Food Inspection Agency using techniques such as spraying, tilling, burning, hand roguing and mowing. Ensuring standard operating procedures are maintained and followed. If you have... Strong agronomic competence attained through pursuing or having obtained your degree in Agriculture or another college along with experience in the Agriculture Industry. Demonstrated problem solving skills. Ability to be flexible and modify plans as situations arise minimizing impact on results. Interest in canola development. Ability to deliver results in pursuit of both individual and team goals. Ability to be comfortable being on the road for extended periods of time, driving highway and grid roads to get to active trial or post harvest sites. About us As the world’s leading chemical company, we have been finding solutions for your everyday needs and addressing the most complex economic, environmental, and sustainability challenges for more than 150 years! We constantly strive to become an even better workplace. As a result of our commitment to our employees, BASF has earned various awards, including being recognized by Mediacorp in 2024 as one of Canada's Top Employers for tenth consecutive year, one of the Greater Toronto Area's Top Employers for the eleventh year in a row, and twice being named one of Canada's Top Employers for Young People and Best Diversity Employers. We provide a fulfilling work environment with a strong emphasis on the physical and psychological safety of our employees and the communities we operate in. We are always working to form the best team with an emphasis on diversity, equity, and inclusion and through lifelong learning and development. Belong to Something Bigger. #belongatBASF Privacy Statement BASF takes security & data privacy very seriously. We will never request personal or financial information of any kind via either text message or direct message on any social media platform or job board. Furthermore, we will never offer a candidate a position and request a cheque for equipment or any other type of payment during the job application process. If you have experienced any of the above, please contact myhr@basf.com to report fraud. Inclusion and Equal Opportunity Employment BASF is dedicated to creating a diverse and inclusive workplace where our employees can be their authentic selves. We are an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, age, citizenship, place of origin, ethnic origin, colour, religion/creed, sex/pregnancy, marital status, family status, disability status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by law. We seek to make our recruitment process accessible for applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodations or support throughout the recruitment process, please contact ta-canada@basf.com with the subject line “Recruitment Accommodation Request”. Job Description Footer:
